page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc8i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6462C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 8 physical cores, resulting in a configuration of 1.0 thread per core. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This 1:2 ratio positions the server within compute-heavy profiles where processing power is prioritized over large memory footprints. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated physical core allocation offers predictable compute performance per resource unit, though the lack of local storage and a lower memory-to-CPU ratio represent clear architectural tradeoffs. This server is designed for workloads that require stable, high-frequency CPU execution without massive memory demands, such as batch processing, compute-intensive web serving, and distributed analytics nodes.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc8i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6462C processor running at 3.3 GHz, it delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It features no local storage and provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without hyperthreading overhead, making it suitable for batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute nodes.
